findings.message: plugin.json description claims 67 styles and 15 stacks but SKILL.md self-reports 50+ styles and 10 stacks; enumerated stacks in description total 12, not 15.
plugin.json marketplace description reports 67 styles and 15 stacks; SKILL.md documents 50+ styles and 10 stacks; enumerated stacks in plugin.json total 12.
Code block references model ID 'gemini-3-pro-image-preview' which is not a confirmed public Gemini model identifier as of audit date; may cause silent generation failures.

banner-design/SKILL.md and design/SKILL.md both depend on ai-artist, ai-multimodal, chrome-devtools, and frontend-design skills that are not declared as dependencies in plugin.json and not present in the plugin install.
Brand skill provides no output format description, no workflow example, and no sample of what brand context extraction looks like — pure routing to reference files.

execSync called with a backtick template string; all three variables are hardcoded constants so no current injection risk, but the pattern is fragile — future user-controlled substitution would create shell injection.

Workflow steps reference ai-artist, ai-multimodal, chrome-devtools, frontend-design, project-management, assets-organizing skills not bundled in this plugin with no fallback instructions for missing dependencies.
Banner workflow (steps 1-5, size table, art styles, design rules) is fully reproduced in design/SKILL.md despite existing in the dedicated banner-design/SKILL.md; divergence risk over time.
Raw template variable left unsubstituted in rendered skill body; renders as literal XML-like tag and may confuse LLM consumers.
Slides skill has no output format, no example of generated slide HTML, and no direct workflow steps; all content is deferred to references/create.md which is not in the plugin manifest.
slides/SKILL.md routes to references/create.md as its primary content, but this file is not included in the plugin install path defined in plugin.json.
Dev dependencies use >= floor constraints with no upper bound (pytest>=8.0.0, pytest-cov>=4.1.0, pytest-mock>=3.12.0); a future breaking major release would be silently pulled in.

subprocess.run passes 'npx shadcn@latest' without a version pin; each invocation silently downloads whatever npm publishes as latest. No shell injection because list form is used, but the package itself is unverified at runtime.
name field is 'ui-ux-pro-max' without the 'ckm:' namespace prefix used by all other skills in this plugin (ckm:brand, ckm:design-system, ckm:ui-styling, etc.).
Step 1 contains 'Stack: React Native (this project's only tech stack)' — a project-scoped note published in a generic skill that otherwise documents 10 stacks. Contradicts the rest of the file.
All skills in plugin.json use the 'ckm:' namespace prefix except ui-ux-pro-max, which registers as a plain 'ui-ux-pro-max' name. Breaks namespace consistency across the plugin.
All four runtime dependencies use caret ranges (^), allowing silent minor/patch updates without explicit approval. Supply chain drift risk over time.

downloadRelease() fetches a ZIP from GitHub releases and writes to disk without checksum verification; init.ts extracts and installs the contents into the user project. A compromised release asset would be silently installed.
Raw template variable left unsubstituted in rendered skill body; renders as literal XML-like tag and may confuse LLM consumers.
